behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in module handoff (parser -> range resolver -> function evaluator -> cell store), not a single pure function. No OS/network/subprocess, no AI model, no multi-agent sequence; behavior text is purely local engine computation.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook in-process, set A1:A10 numeric values, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)` in another cell, and assert computed value and cached value consistency after one recompute pass.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This specifically tests cross-cell formula chaining and state handoff across dependency/evaluation modules; unit-only tests can miss graph/eval integration defects. No real infra or AI dependency is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1`, `B1 (=A1*2)`, and `C1 (=B1+1)` in one workbook, run recomputation, and assert final values and dependency links are consistent.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection correctness depends on interactions between reference graph construction and evaluator control flow. Behavior text does not require OS/subprocess/network round-trip, nor any model output.
behaviors.test_description: Build a sheet with `A1 = B1+1` and `B1 = A1+1`, run evaluation, assert deterministic circular-reference error object/code and bounded execution (no unbounded iteration).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Needs parser + range extraction + function dispatch + error propagation working together. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is local formula evaluation with no external contract/infra.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range, evaluate one exact-match VLOOKUP and one missing-key VLOOKUP, assert returned value for hit and expected not-found error for miss.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Risk is expression parsing/evaluation across nested function calls and condition branches, which is multi-module engine behavior. No explicit real infra or AI dependence in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF formulas over representative `A1` values (<=10, 11..20, >20) and assert branch outputs and type handling match expected strings.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on cell reference resolution plus function semantics, not only one isolated function. System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by absence of external infra or model-driven decisions in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Set referenced cells with text and empties, evaluate CONCATENATE formula including separators, and assert final string omits empty-cell placeholders.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet lookup is a module-integration concern (sheet namespace resolution + evaluator). No real OS/network/subprocess or AI output is required by the behavior statement.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with `Sheet2!A1` set, evaluate `=Sheet2!A1+1` from another sheet, and assert resolved value and sheet lookup path are correct.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-propagation inside local modules (dependency graph + invalidation + recompute scheduler). No SYSTEM triad trigger is explicit; no AI/model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency DAG across multiple cells, mutate an upstream cell, trigger recompute, and assert both final values and recomputation order respect topological constraints.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness requires interaction among edit history, formula store, dependency graph, and recalculation. Behavior text is in-process state restoration, with no external infra or AI calls.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula that affects downstream cells, capture pre-edit state, perform edit and recompute, execute undo, then assert formula text and all dependent values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires proving an externally visible side effect via disk round-trip readback (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). Integration with fake serialization can miss real file boundary issues; no AI/workflow aspects apply.
behaviors.test_description: Write workbook to a real temporary file using production binary serializer, load it through the production loader, and assert exact formula text equality plus numeric equality within strict precision policy for all cells.
